summary: Fix authentication flow on mobile app (iOS 17+)
spec:
Users on iOS 17+ are unable to complete login due to a WebKit restriction introduced in the latest OS update. The biometric authentication flow breaks silently and the user is left on a blank screen.

Expected: User taps login → biometric prompt → authenticated session.
Actual: User taps login → blank screen after biometric.
plan:
1. Investigate iOS 17 WebKit SameSite cookie restrictions
2. Update /auth/login endpoint to return token in Authorization header instead of cookie
3. Patch static/app.js to store and send Bearer token
4. Add CORS headers for mobile WebView origins
5. Write regression tests covering the mobile login flow
tasks:
- [x] Patch login endpoint for iOS 17 cookie compatibility
- [x] Update static/app.js auth handler to use Bearer token
- [x] Add CORS middleware for mobile origins
- [x] Write tests for mobile auth flow
- [x] Update requirements.txt with python-jose bump
solution:
Root cause: iOS 17 WebKit now blocks SameSite=Lax cookies in cross-origin WebView contexts.

Fix applied:
- Switched from session cookies to short-lived JWT Bearer tokens returned in the response body
- Updated static/app.js to store token in sessionStorage and attach it as Authorization: Bearer <token> on every request
- Added FastAPI CORS middleware entry for app.forgex.io mobile origin
- Bumped python-jose to 3.3.0 for the latest JWT fixes

All 14 auth tests passing. Verified on iOS 17.4 simulator.
